```{r surv-flow-scatter, fig.height=5}
               hjust = 0, vjust = 1, size = 2.8, colour = "grey30") +
      scale_y_continuous(
        labels = scales::number_format(accuracy = 0.01),
        limits = c(
          max(0,   min(scurve_df$Lo90,  splot_df$Surv_adj_lo, na.rm = TRUE) - 0.05),
          min(1,   max(scurve_df$Hi90,  splot_df$Surv_adj_hi, na.rm = TRUE) + 0.05)
        )
      ) +
      scale_x_log10(labels = scales::comma) +
      labs(
        title    = paste0(SS, "  |  Median survival = ", round(med_surv_prob, 3)),
        subtitle = paste0(
          season_sv_label, " flow (lagged 0 or 1 year depending on flow season and sampling season). ",
          "Points = density-adjusted survival (b6S\u00d7NALL[t-1]/100 removed). ",
          "Curve = fitted at median NALL. ",
          "Red = below-median survival. ",
          paste0("Dot-dash = median survival = ", round(med_surv_prob, 3), ". "),
          "Grey band = 90% CrI."),
        x = paste0(season_sv_label, " Flow (cfs, log scale)"),
        y = "Annual Survival (probability)"
      ) +
      theme_report() +
      theme(plot.title = element_text(size = 11, face = "bold"))
  }

} else {
  cat("*Surv posterior or flow data not available for this site.*")
}
```

---

# 6. Explained Residual Variance

Proportion of residual variance in recruitment (σ_R) and survival (σ_Surv) explained by the flow model relative to a null (no-flow) model.

**Formula:** Explained variance = 1 − (σ_flow / σ_null)

Positive values indicate the flow model reduces unexplained variation; negative values indicate the flow model inflates it.

```{r explained-variance-table}
ev_rows <- bind_rows(
  if (!is.null(ev_rec))  fmt_row("Recruitment (\u03c3_R)",  ev_rec,  digits = 3),
  if (!is.null(ev_surv)) fmt_row("Survival (\u03c3_Surv)",  ev_surv, digits = 3)
)

if (nrow(ev_rows) > 0) {
  ev_rows %>%
    kable(caption = paste("Explained residual variance \u2014 flow vs. null model \u2014", SS),
          align   = c("l", "r", "l")) %>%
    kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover"),
                  full_width = FALSE) %>%
    row_spec(0, bold = TRUE, background = "#2C5F8A", color = "white") %>%
    column_spec(2, bold = TRUE, color = "#1A5276")
} else {
  cat("*Residual-variance comparison not available (missing null or flow model).*")
}
```

```{r explained-variance-plot, fig.height=4}
ev_list <- list()
if (!is.null(ev_rec))  ev_list[["Recruitment (sigma_R)"]]  <- ev_rec
if (!is.null(ev_surv)) ev_list[["Survival (sigma_Surv)"]] <- ev_surv

if (length(ev_list) > 0) {
  map_dfr(names(ev_list), ~ tibble(Process = .x, EV = ev_list[[.x]])) %>%
    ggplot(aes(x = EV, fill = Process, color = Process)) +
    geom_density(alpha = 0.4, linewidth = 0.9) +
    geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "gray30") +
    scale_fill_manual(values  = c("Recruitment (sigma_R)"  = "#E74C3C",
                                  "Survival (sigma_Surv)"  = "#27AE60")) +
    scale_color_manual(values = c("Recruitment (sigma_R)"  = "#C0392B",
                                  "Survival (sigma_Surv)"  = "#1E8449")) +
    labs(title    = paste(SS, "\u2014 Posterior: Explained Residual Variance"),
         subtitle = "1 \u2212 (\u03c3_flow / \u03c3_null). Dashed line = 0 (no improvement).",
         x = "Explained Variance", y = "Density", fill = NULL, color = NULL) +
    theme_report() + theme(legend.position = "bottom")
} else {
  cat("*No explained-variance posteriors to plot.*")
}
```

```{r sigma-comparison-plot, fig.height=4}
sigma_df_list <- list()

if (!is.null(sigmaR_null) && !is.null(sigmaR_flow))
  sigma_df_list[["Recruitment (\u03c3_R)"]] <- tibble(
    Value = c(sigmaR_null, sigmaR_flow),
    Model = rep(c("Null", "Flow"), c(length(sigmaR_null), length(sigmaR_flow)))
  )
if (!is.null(sigmaSurv_null) && !is.null(sigmaSurv_flow))
  sigma_df_list[["Survival (\u03c3_Surv)"]] <- tibble(
    Value = c(sigmaSurv_null, sigmaSurv_flow),
    Model = rep(c("Null", "Flow"), c(length(sigmaSurv_null), length(sigmaSurv_flow)))
  )

if (length(sigma_df_list) > 0) {
  bind_rows(sigma_df_list, .id = "Process") %>%
    mutate(Model = factor(Model, levels = c("Null", "Flow"))) %>%
    ggplot(aes(x = Value, fill = Model, color = Model)) +
    geom_density(alpha = 0.4, linewidth = 0.8) +
    scale_fill_manual(values  = c("Null" = "#AAB7B8", "Flow" = "#2E86C1")) +
    scale_color_manual(values = c("Null" = "#717D7E", "Flow" = "#1A5276")) +
    facet_wrap(~ Process, scales = "free", ncol = 2) +
    labs(title    = paste(SS, "\u2014 Sigma Posteriors: Flow Model vs. Null Model"),
         subtitle = "Leftward shift in Flow model indicates improved fit.",
         x = "\u03c3", y = "Density", fill = NULL, color = NULL) +
    theme_report() + theme(legend.position = "bottom")
} else {
  cat("*Sigma comparison not available.*")
}
```

---

# Appendix: Full Model Selection Details

This appendix documents the full model-selection process used to arrive at the top-selected flow structure reported above. The IPM was fit repeatedly across all candidate flow-lag combinations and linear/quadratic shape combinations for each demographic process (recruitment and survival). Bayesian indicator variables track how often each lag and each quadratic term is included in the posterior, providing a natural measure of support.

## Lag Inclusion Probabilities

Posterior probability that each candidate flow lag is included in the model, by sub-model type. Bars to the right of the dashed line indicate support greater than the equal prior probability; the selected top lag for each sub-model is highlighted.

```{r appendix-lag-bar, fig.height=4.5}
ggplot(lag_summary,
       aes(x = reorder(LagLabel, AllInclusion), y = AllInclusion, fill = IsTop)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.7, color = "white") +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 1/3, linetype = "dashed", color = "gray50") +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c("FALSE" = "#A8C8E8", "TRUE" = "#1A5276"),
    labels = c("FALSE" = "Other lags", "TRUE" = "Top lag (selected)"),
    name   = NULL
  ) +
  facet_wrap(~ RowModelType, scales = "free_x") +
  labs(
    title    = paste(SS, "\u2014 Lag Inclusion Probabilities"),
    subtitle = "Dashed line = equal prior probability (1/3).",
    x        = "Flow Lag  (S = summer years prior, W = winter years prior)",
    y        = "Inclusion Probability"
  ) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 11) +
  theme(
    strip.background = element_rect(fill = "#2C5F8A"),
    strip.text       = element_text(color = "white", face = "bold"),
    axis.text.x      = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    legend.position  = "bottom",
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
  )
```

```{r appendix-lag-heatmap, fig.height=4, eval=has_global}
# Heatmap only renders for global model sites
ggplot(lag_summary %>% filter(RowModelType == "Global (Summer + Winter)"),
       aes(x = factor(WinterLag), y = factor(SummerLag), fill = AllInclusion)) +
  geom_tile(color = "white", linewidth = 1) +
  geom_text(aes(label = sprintf("%.3f", AllInclusion)),
            size = 4, color = "white", fontface = "bold") +
  scale_fill_gradient2(
    low      = "#EBF5FB",
    mid      = "#2E86C1",
    high     = "#1A5276",
    midpoint = 1/9,
    name     = "Inclusion\nPr."
  ) +
  labs(
    title    = paste(SS, "\u2014 Joint Summer \u00d7 Winter Lag Inclusion (Global Model)"),
    subtitle = "Equal prior = 1/9.",
    x        = "Winter Flow Lag (years prior)",
    y        = "Summer Flow Lag (years prior)"
  ) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 11) +
  theme(panel.grid = element_blank())
```

## Quadratic Term Inclusion — All Lag Combinations

These panels show the probability of preferring a quadratic over a linear flow–response for each demographic process, across every evaluated lag combination. Points are shaded by lag inclusion probability; the red-bordered point marks the top-selected lag for that sub-model.

```{r appendix-quad-across-lags, fig.height=6}
quad_long <- site_data %>%
  distinct(RowModelType, SummerLag, WinterLag, AllInclusion,
           quadInclusionRecSUM, quadInclusionRecWINT,
           quadInclusionSurvSUM, quadInclusionSurvWINT) %>%
  left_join(lag_summary %>% select(RowModelType, SummerLag, WinterLag,
                                   LagLabel, IsTop),
            by = c("RowModelType", "SummerLag", "WinterLag")) %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols      = starts_with("quadInclusion"),
    names_to  = "Process",
    values_to = "QuadProb"
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(QuadProb)) %>%
  mutate(
    ProcessLabel = recode(Process,
      "quadInclusionRecSUM"   = "Summer \u2192 Recruitment",
      "quadInclusionRecWINT"  = "Winter \u2192 Recruitment",
      "quadInclusionSurvSUM"  = "Summer \u2192 Survival",
      "quadInclusionSurvWINT" = "Winter \u2192 Survival"
    )
  )

ggplot(quad_long,
       aes(x = LagLabel, y = QuadProb,
           fill = AllInclusion, color = IsTop, size = IsTop)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0.5, linetype = "dashed", color = "gray60") +
  geom_point(shape = 21, stroke = 1.2) +
  scale_fill_gradient(low = "#D6EAF8", high = "#1A5276",
                      name = "Lag inclusion\nprobability") +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("FALSE" = "gray70", "TRUE" = "#E74C3C"),
                     labels = c("FALSE" = "Other lags", "TRUE" = "Top lag"),
                     name = NULL) +
  scale_size_manual(values = c("FALSE" = 3, "TRUE" = 5), guide = "none") +
  facet_wrap(~ ProcessLabel, ncol = 2) +
  labs(
    title    = paste(SS, "\u2014 Quadratic Inclusion Probability by Lag & Process"),
    subtitle = "Red border = selected top lag. Dashed line = 0.5.",
    x        = "Lag Combination",
    y        = "P(Quadratic term included)"
  ) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 10) +
  theme(
    strip.background = element_rect(fill = "#2C5F8A"),
    strip.text       = element_text(color = "white", face = "bold"),
    axis.text.x      = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    legend.position  = "right",
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
  )
```

## Full Inclusion Probability Table

All lag \u00d7 quadratic combinations, sorted by lag inclusion probability then quadratic combination inclusion probability.

```{r appendix-full-table}
site_data %>%
  arrange(RowModelType, desc(AllInclusion), desc(lagInclusion)) %>%
  transmute(
    `Sub-model`    = RowModelType,
    `Summer Lag`   = ifelse(is.na(SummerLag), "\u2014", as.character(SummerLag)),
    `Winter Lag`   = ifelse(is.na(WinterLag), "\u2014", as.character(WinterLag)),
    `Lag Pr.`      = round(AllInclusion, 4),
    `Sum Rec Q`    = ifelse(is.na(SummerRecQuad),  "\u2014", SummerRecQuad),
    `Sum Surv Q`   = ifelse(is.na(SummerSurvQuad), "\u2014", SummerSurvQuad),
    `Win Rec Q`    = ifelse(is.na(WinterRecQuad),  "\u2014", WinterRecQuad),
    `Win Surv Q`   = ifelse(is.na(WinterSurvQuad), "\u2014", WinterSurvQuad),
    `Quad Combo Pr.` = round(lagInclusion, 4),
    `Q Pr Sum Rec`   = round(quadInclusionRecSUM,  3),
    `Q Pr Win Rec`   = round(quadInclusionRecWINT, 3),
    `Q Pr Sum Surv`  = round(quadInclusionSurvSUM, 3),
    `Q Pr Win Surv`  = round(quadInclusionSurvWINT, 3)
  ) %>%
  kable(caption = paste("All lag/quadratic combinations \u2014", SS)) %>%
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped","hover","condensed"),
                font_size = 9, full_width = TRUE) %>%
  row_spec(0, bold = TRUE, background = "#2C5F8A", color = "white")
```

---

```{=html}
<script>
// Toggle summer/winter flow bars on the abundance single plot.
// Traces per age group (5 each, 4 groups): ribbon, line, pts, sum_bar, win_bar.
// Summer bars at 0-based indices 3,8,13,18; winter at 4,9,14,19.
// We only make a bar visible if its parent age group is also currently visible
// (checked via the ribbon trace visibility for each group).
function toggleAbundFlow() {
  var el = document.getElementById('abund-plot');
  if (!el) return;
  var sum = document.getElementById('cb_sum_abund').checked;
  var win = document.getElementById('cb_win_abund').checked;
  var nGroups = 4;
  var sumIdx = [], winIdx = [], sumVis = [], winVis = [];
  for (var g = 0; g < nGroups; g++) {
    var ribbonVis = el.data[g * 5].visible;   // ribbon = first trace of each group
    var groupOn   = (ribbonVis === true);
    sumIdx.push(g * 5 + 3);
    winIdx.push(g * 5 + 4);
    sumVis.push(groupOn && sum ? true : false);
    winVis.push(groupOn && win ? true : false);
  }
  Plotly.restyle(el, {visible: sumVis}, sumIdx);
  Plotly.restyle(el, {visible: winVis}, winIdx);
}

// Toggle flow bars on the survival plot (trace 3=summer, 4=winter).
function toggleSurvFlow() {
  var el = document.getElementById('surv-plot');
  if (!el) return;
  var sum = document.getElementById('cb_sum_surv').checked;
  var win = document.getElementById('cb_win_surv').checked;
  Plotly.restyle(el, {visible: sum ? true : false}, [3]);
  Plotly.restyle(el, {visible: win ? true : false}, [4]);
}
</script>
```


<!-- # Session Info -->

<!-- ```{r session-info} -->
<!-- sessionInfo() -->
<!-- ``` -->
